zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy de Norton於大約1310年出生在英國哈普爾時，她的父親Jacob de Norton26歲，母親Elizabeth de la Stane42歲。她和William de Weston至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1344年之後在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy de Norton于大约1310年出生在英国哈普爾时，她的父亲Jacob de Norton26岁，母亲Elizabeth de la Stane42岁。她和William de Weston至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1344年之后在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Norton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places so called, from Old English north ‘north’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. In some cases it is a variant of Norrington .
Irish: altered form of   Naughton  , assimilated to the English name (see 1 above).
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname.

variants.name: Norrington
meanings: (southeastern): from Norringtonend in Redbourn (Hertforshire), Northingtown Farm in Grimley (Worcestershire), or Norrington in Alvediston (Wiltshire). All three names are from Old English north in tūne ‘to the north of the village’.
possibly a variant of Northampton, a habitational name from the city of this name, which was named with Old English north ‘north’ + hām + tūn ‘homestead, home farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Amy de Norton was born about 1310, in Hampshire, England, her father, Jacob de Norton, was 26 and her mother, Elizabeth de la Stane, was 42. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with William de Weston. She died after 1344, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amy de Norton est née environ 1310, à Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Jacob de Norton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de la Stane, avait 42 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec William de Weston. Elle est décédée après 1344, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Amy de Norton um 1310 in Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jacob de Norton 26 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de la Stane 42. Sie hatte mit William de Weston mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1344 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Amy de Norton è nata il circa 1310 nel Hampshire, England, sua padre, Jacob de Norton, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de la Stane, aveva 42 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con William de Weston. Morì dopo il 1344, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy de Nortonが約1310年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Jacob de Nortonは26歳，母Elizabeth de la Staneは42歳であった。彼女はWilliam de Westonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1344年以後にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amy de Norton(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 함프실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jacob de Norton(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de la Stane(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 William de Weston(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 이후 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Amy de Norton nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Jacob de Norton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de la Stane, tinha 42 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com William de Weston. Ela faleceu após 1344, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy de Norton родилась около 1310 г. в местности хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Jacob de Norton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de la Stane, 42. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от William de Weston. Она умерла после 1344 г. в местности Англия.

sm.about 1310–after 1344: e tusa o le 1310–ina ua ma'ea le 1344

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy de Norton nació aproximadamente 1310, en Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Jacob de Norton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de la Stane, tenía 42 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con William de Weston. Murió después de 1344, en Inglaterra.
